verbatim_map.@@verbatim:2:04c3c8@@:
<!--
			Topology is, in essence, the study of the properties that 
			are preserved through continuous deformations of "objects",
			such as twisting, stretching or bending, but not tearing or 
			gluing.

The "objects" of study in topology are formally defined as topological spaces,
which are sets endowed with a structure, called a topology. 
This topology allows to define continuous deformations of 
subspaces, and, more generally, all kinds of continuity.

In particulartopology In the case of the set of complex numbers $\mathbb C$
The "" In topology the main objects of study are called topological spaces.  
We can think of topological spaces as sets of points together with some 
notion of continuity. This notion of continuity, in this case, comes in the form
of a mathematical gadget called topology that determines which functions mapping out

The technical definition of topology is a bit unintuitive.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=pSjahcOnJvU
                        -->

verbatim_map.@@math:36:46fd45@@: $\text{Ext } S=\left\{z: z \text{ is an exterior point of } S \right\}$
verbatim_map.@@math:37:c6438d@@: $\partial S=\left\{z: z \text{ is a boundary point of } S \right\}$
